name: Zazaki
notes: Zazaki (Dimli / Kirmanjki), a Northwestern Iranian language of eastern Anatolia. COMMITMENT: this map implements the 32-letter ZAZA LATIN ALPHABET derived from the Kurdish Hawar (Bedirxan) alphabet — the alphabet used by the Zazaki Wikipedia and by modern Zazaki publishing. INPUT CONTRACT: lowercase Latin-script Zazaki with its diacritics intact (ç ê î ş û, and ğ/ẍ, ı/i). The Perso-Arabic writing tradition (an abjad) is OUT OF SCOPE. Do NOT cross-apply Kurmanji Hawar values: in Zaza, <u> = /ʊ/ and <û> = /u/, <î> = /i/, <i>/<ı> = /ɪ/, <e> = /ɛ/ and <ê> = /e/ — there are no phonemic long vowels marked by the circumflex. <ğ> (also written <ẍ>) = /ɣ/. <c> = /d͡ʒ/, <ç> = /t͡ʃ/, <j> = /ʒ/, <ş> = /ʃ/, <x> = /x/, <q> = /q/, <y> = /j/, <w> = /w/. NOT WRITTEN: the pharyngeal and pharyngealised consonants of the phoneme inventory (/ħ ʕ tˁ sˤ/) and the velarised lateral /ɫ/ have no distinct letters, and vowel nasalisation before /n/ is allophonic; these are carried in the allophone table, not recoverable from the spelling.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors

sources.notes: Carries the 32-letter Zaza Latin alphabet with its IPA row, and the phoneme inventory / allophony used here.

sources.title: A Grammar of Dimili (also known as Zaza)

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Kurdish alphabets
sources.notes: The Hawar (Bedirxan) alphabet the Zaza Latin alphabet derives from; its DIFFERENT vowel values are noted in this spec so they are not cross-applied.
